Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

DIMENSION: public API & adapter-Protocol stability. A clone depends on the contracts (parser/claims/features/study Protocols, BaselineBuilder, run_study/StudyPlan/StudyResult signatures, the top-level public API). Find: breaking inconsistencies, Protocols that can't be implemented as documented, missing runtime checks that a misimplemented adapter would need, signature/return-type surprises, things that would silently break a study on a minor engine change. Check the @runtime_checkable usage and isinstance behavior.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Let me read the core contracts and public API.

content.content:
1	"""assay_engine — the reusable, dataset-agnostic engine of the hc-assay blueprint.
2	
3	The single architectural rule (ADR-0002, ARCHITECTURE.md §3):
4	
5	    The engine never imports dataset specifics. Adapters implement the interfaces in
6	    ``assay_engine.contracts`` that the engine calls. A clone is: implement the adapter,
7	    register it, run.
8	
9	This package contains only goal-agnostic machinery:
10	
11	- ``contracts``      — the engine ↔ adapter boundary (canonical schema + adapter Protocols).
12	- ``methodology``    — hypotheses, the three verdicts, the two firewalls, the
13	                       measurement↔interpretation fence, confirmatory-test machinery.
14	- ``baseline``       — dataset-agnostic baseline builders (embeddings, similarity, graph,
15	                       clustering, descriptive stats).
16	- ``orchestration``  — the analysis graph, phase machine, and governance gates.
17	- ``reasoning``      — the tiered LLM reasoning seam.
18	- ``observability``  — self-hosted tracing + experiment tracking.
19	- ``persistence``    — durable checkpointing, data versioning, vector store.
20	- ``provenance``     — the append-only, hash-chained audit trail (GOVERNANCE §3).
21	- ``pipeline``       — ``run_study``: the composed, governed end-to-end runner (ADR-0010).
22	- ``registry``       — adapter registration.
23	
24	Nothing in this package may import an adapter module or name a specific dataset, authority,
25	or taxonomy.
26	"""
27	
28	__version__ = "0.0.1"
29	
30	# Public API — the surface a cloning study uses. Heavy/optional backends stay behind their
31	# subpackages (imported lazily there); these names are dependency-free to import.
32	from assay_engine.contracts import (  # noqa: E402
33	    ClaimRecord,
34	    Corpus,
35	    ExternalClaimsSource,
36	    FeatureBuilder,
37	    FeatureMatrix,
38	    IngestionParser,
39	    Relation,
40	    StudyDefinition,
41	    StudyMode,
42	    Unit,
43	)
44	from assay_engine.pipeline import (  # noqa: E402
45	    GateHandler,
46	    GateReview,
47	    IngestionError,
48	    StudyPlan,
49	    StudyResult,
50	    auto_approve,
51	    run_study,
52	)
53	from assay_engine.provenance import (  # noqa: E402
54	    ProvenanceEntry,
55	    ProvenanceError,
56	    ProvenanceTrail,
57	    from_records,
58	    verify_records,
59	)
60	from assay_engine.registry import (  # noqa: E402
61	    get_study,
62	    register_study,
63	    registered_studies,
64	)
65	
66	__all__ = [
67	    "__version__",
68	    # contracts
69	    "ClaimRecord",
70	    "Corpus",
71	    "ExternalClaimsSource",
72	    "FeatureBuilder",
73	    "FeatureMatrix",
74	    "IngestionParser",
75	    "Relation",
76	    "StudyDefinition",
77	    "StudyMode",
78	    "Unit",
79	    # pipeline
80	    "GateHandler",
81	    "GateReview",
82	    "IngestionError",
83	    "StudyPlan",
84	    "StudyResult",
85	    "auto_approve",
86	    "run_study",
87	    # provenance
88	    "ProvenanceEntry",
89	    "ProvenanceError",
90	    "ProvenanceTrail",
91	    "from_records",
92	    "verify_records",
93	    # registry
94	    "get_study",
95	    "register_study",
96	    "registered_studies",
97	]
98

1	"""Ingestion parser interface — the only adapter piece that understands the raw format.
2	
3	An adapter implements :class:`IngestionParser` to turn its native source into a canonical
4	:class:`~assay_engine.contracts.schema.Corpus`. The engine calls ``parse`` and works only
5	with the canonical result.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	from pathlib import Path
11	from typing import Protocol
12	
13	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
14	
15	
16	class IngestionParser(Protocol):
17	    """Raw source → canonical :class:`Corpus`.
18	
19	    Implementations live in adapters. They must be deterministic: the same source bytes
20	    produce the same ``Corpus`` (so the baseline is reproducible). Implementations must not
21	    read any external-claims artifact here — claims load through a separate provider.
22	    """
23	
24	    def parse(self, source: Path) -> Corpus:
25	        """Parse ``source`` into a canonical corpus."""
26	        ...
27	
28	    def source_fingerprint(self, source: Path) -> str:
29	        """Return a stable content hash of ``source`` for provenance and determinism checks."""
30	        ...
31

1	"""External-claims source interface — optional, and quarantined from the baseline.
2	
3	If a dataset ships claims asserted by some external authority (relationships, labels, a
4	taxonomy, mappings), an adapter exposes them through :class:`ExternalClaimsSource`. The
5	engine converts each :class:`ClaimRecord` into a typed, falsifiable hypothesis and tests it
6	against the baseline — which is kept blind to these claims (Firewall A).
7	
8	This interface is *structurally separable*: the baseline pipeline never receives it, so the
9	baseline can be produced with claims withheld. Pure-discovery datasets omit it entirely.
10	Access to a claims source while claim-blindness is in force raises ``FirewallViolation``
11	(see :mod:`assay_engine.methodology.firewalls`).
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
17	from typing import Any, Iterable, Mapping, Protocol
18	
19	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
20	
21	
22	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
23	class ClaimRecord:
24	    """One external, expert-asserted claim about the data — a falsifiable assertion.
25	
26	    The engine treats this as a *hypothesis source*, never as ground truth. ``subject`` and
27	    ``referents`` name the units the claim is about; ``assertion`` describes what is claimed
28	    in a typed, machine-readable way; ``provenance`` records who asserted it and where from.
29	    """
30	
31	    claim_id: str
32	    subject: str
33	    referents: tuple[str, ...]
34	    assertion: Mapping[str, Any]
35	    provenance: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
36	
37	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
38	        object.__setattr__(self, "assertion", freeze_mapping(self.assertion))
39	        object.__setattr__(self, "provenance", freeze_mapping(self.provenance))
40	
41	
42	class ExternalClaimsSource(Protocol):
43	    """Adapter-provided access to a dataset's external claims (adjudication mode only)."""
44	
45	    def claims(self) -> Iterable[ClaimRecord]:
46	        """Yield every external claim to be adjudicated against the blind baseline."""
47	        ...
48	
49	    def claim_fingerprint(self) -> str:
50	        """Stable content hash of the claim set, for provenance and pre-registration."""
51	        ...
52

1	"""Feature-builder interface — optional dataset-specific features.
2	
3	The baseline toolkit provides generic builders (embeddings, similarity, etc.). When a
4	dataset has features only meaningful in its own domain, an adapter supplies them through a
5	:class:`FeatureBuilder`. Output is a plain numeric :class:`FeatureMatrix` keyed by unit id,
6	so the engine consumes it without knowing what the features mean.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	from dataclasses import dataclass
12	from typing import Protocol, Sequence
13	
14	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
15	
16	
17	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
18	class FeatureMatrix:
19	    """Numeric features for a set of units. ``rows[i]`` corresponds to ``unit_ids[i]``."""
20	
21	    unit_ids: tuple[str, ...]
22	    feature_names: tuple[str, ...]
23	    rows: tuple[tuple[float, ...], ...]
24	
25	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
26	        if len(self.rows) != len(self.unit_ids):
27	            raise ValueError("FeatureMatrix row count must match unit_ids length")
28	        width = len(self.feature_names)
29	        if any(len(r) != width for r in self.rows):
30	            raise ValueError("FeatureMatrix rows must match feature_names width")
31	
32	
33	class FeatureBuilder(Protocol):
34	    """Build dataset-specific numeric features from a canonical corpus."""
35	
36	    def build(self, corpus: Corpus) -> FeatureMatrix:
37	        """Compute features for (a subset of) the corpus units. Must be deterministic."""
38	        ...
39	
40	    @property
41	    def provides(self) -> Sequence[str]:
42	        """Names of the features this builder produces (for provenance)."""
43	        ...
44

1	"""Study definition — the adapter's research questions and pre-registration handle.
2	
3	A study definition binds an adapter's pieces together for one concrete run: which parser,
4	which mode, the optional claims source, any extra feature builders, and the research
5	questions. It lives in the instance repository (one repo per study, ADR-0002), not in the
6	engine. The engine reads it to drive the run and to anchor pre-registration.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	from dataclasses import dataclass
12	from enum import Enum
13	from typing import Sequence
14	
15	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ExternalClaimsSource
16	from assay_engine.contracts.features import FeatureBuilder
17	from assay_engine.contracts.parser import IngestionParser
18	
19	
20	class StudyMode(Enum):
21	    """The two modes from the charter. A study may combine both."""
22	
23	    DISCOVERY = "discovery"
24	    ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS = "adjudicate_external_claims"
25	
26	
27	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
28	class StudyDefinition:
29	    """Everything the engine needs to run one study, supplied by an adapter."""
30	
31	    name: str
32	    modes: frozenset[StudyMode]
33	    parser: IngestionParser
34	    research_questions: tuple[str, ...]
35	    feature_builders: tuple[FeatureBuilder, ...] = ()
36	    claims_source: ExternalClaimsSource | None = None
37	
38	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
39	        if not self.name.strip():
40	            raise ValueError("StudyDefinition.name must be non-empty (it is the registry key)")
41	        if not self.modes:
42	            raise ValueError("StudyDefinition must declare at least one mode")
43	        needs_claims = StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in self.modes
44	        if needs_claims and self.claims_source is None:
45	            raise ValueError("Adjudication mode requires a claims_source; none was provided")
46	        if not needs_claims and self.claims_source is not None:
47	            # A discovery-only study must not carry a claims source — it would risk
48	            # leaking external judgments into a pipeline that is meant to be blind.
49	            raise ValueError(
50	                "claims_source supplied but ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS mode not declared"
51	            )
52	
53	    @staticmethod
54	    def discovery(
55	        name: str,
56	        parser: IngestionParser,
57	        research_questions: Sequence[str],
58	        feature_builders: Sequence[FeatureBuilder] = (),
59	    ) -> "StudyDefinition":
60	        return StudyDefinition(
61	            name=name,
62	            modes=frozenset({StudyMode.DISCOVERY}),
63	            parser=parser,
64	            research_questions=tuple(research_questions),
65	            feature_builders=tuple(feature_builders),
66	        )
67

1	"""Canonical schema — the dataset-agnostic shape the baseline is built from.
2	
3	An adapter's job is to turn its raw source into a :class:`Corpus` of these types. The engine
4	builds the baseline from a ``Corpus`` and never sees the source's native format.
5	
6	Firewall A boundary (an adapter obligation, not a type-enforced guarantee):
7	    A ``Corpus`` carries the *data itself* — units of text/attributes and any structure that
8	    is intrinsic to the raw source. It must **not** carry an external authority's *judgments*
9	    about the data (asserted relationships, strengths, labels, a taxonomy). Those are claims;
10	    they travel through :mod:`assay_engine.contracts.claims`, quarantined from baseline
11	    construction. Putting an external mapping into ``Corpus.relations`` (or external labels
12	    into ``attributes``/``metadata``) is a Firewall A violation.
13	
14	    Because the mapping/relation payloads are deliberately open (the engine is
15	    dataset-agnostic), this boundary is an **adapter contract obligation** the type system
16	    cannot enforce — not a structural guarantee. The structural guarantees of Firewall A are
17	    that the baseline builder is never handed an ``ExternalClaimsSource`` and that
18	    ``StudyDefinition`` refuses a claims source outside adjudication mode (audit pass 1,
19	    issues #1, #3).
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
25	from typing import Any, Mapping
26	
27	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
28	
29	
30	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
31	class Unit:
32	    """One analyzable object in the corpus (a document, record, entity, item).
33	
34	    ``unit_id`` is stable and unique within the corpus. ``text`` is the natural-language
35	    content the NLP baseline operates on (may be empty for non-text units). ``attributes``
36	    holds structured fields intrinsic to the data — never an external authority's verdicts.
37	    """
38	
39	    unit_id: str
40	    text: str = ""
41	    attributes: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
42	    source_ref: str | None = None
43	
44	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
45	        object.__setattr__(self, "attributes", freeze_mapping(self.attributes))
46	
47	
48	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
49	class Relation:
50	    """A relationship that is *intrinsic to the raw data* (not an external judgment).
51	
52	    Example of legitimate use: a parent/child link the source file itself encodes. Example of
53	    illegitimate use: an external expert's asserted mapping between units — that is a claim
54	    (see :mod:`assay_engine.contracts.claims`) and must be withheld from the baseline.
55	    """
56	
57	    source_id: str
58	    target_id: str
59	    kind: str
60	    attributes: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
61	
62	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
63	        object.__setattr__(self, "attributes", freeze_mapping(self.attributes))
64	
65	
66	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
67	class Corpus:
68	    """The full canonical dataset handed to the baseline builder."""
69	
70	    units: tuple[Unit, ...]
71	    relations: tuple[Relation, ...] = ()
72	    metadata: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
73	
74	    def unit_ids(self) -> frozenset[str]:
75	        return frozenset(u.unit_id for u in self.units)
76	
77	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
78	        ids = [u.unit_id for u in self.units]
79	        if len(ids) != len(set(ids)):
80	            raise ValueError("Corpus contains duplicate unit_id values")
81	        # A closed canonical corpus must not contain relations to non-existent units
82	        # (audit pass 1, issue #3) — mirrors the duplicate-id and FeatureMatrix checks.
83	        known = set(ids)
84	        dangling = sorted(
85	            {e for r in self.relations for e in (r.source_id, r.target_id) if e not in known}
86	        )
87	        if dangling:
88	            raise ValueError(f"Corpus has relations referencing unknown unit_id(s): {dangling[:5]}")
89	        object.__setattr__(self, "metadata", freeze_mapping(self.metadata))
90

1	"""The engine ↔ adapter contract.
2	
3	This subpackage is the stable boundary of the blueprint. The engine depends *only* on the
4	types and Protocols defined here; adapters implement them. Per ARCHITECTURE.md §3:
5	
6	- The engine imports no adapter module.
7	- Adapters depend on the engine, never the reverse, and never on each other.
8	- The external-claims source is structurally separable, so the baseline pipeline can run
9	  with it withheld — that is how Firewall A (claim-blindness) is enforced, not just
10	  promised.
11	"""

13	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord, ExternalClaimsSource
14	from assay_engine.contracts.features import FeatureBuilder, FeatureMatrix

1	"""Adapter registration.
2	
3	A clone registers its :class:`~assay_engine.contracts.study.StudyDefinition` factory under a
4	name; the engine looks it up to run. This is the only place adapters and the engine meet by
5	name, and even here the engine holds only a factory callable — it imports no adapter module
6	itself (ARCHITECTURE.md §3). The instance repository imports the engine, builds its study,
7	and registers it.
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import threading
13	from typing import Callable, Dict
14	
15	from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition
16	
17	StudyFactory = Callable[[], StudyDefinition]
18	
19	_REGISTRY: Dict[str, StudyFactory] = {}
20	# Registration is normally a one-shot import-time op, but the check-then-act duplicate guard
21	# is a TOCTOU; a lock makes it atomic if studies are ever registered concurrently (issue #13).
22	_LOCK = threading.Lock()
23	
24	
25	def register_study(name: str, factory: StudyFactory) -> None:
26	    """Register a study factory under ``name``. Raises on duplicate names."""
27	    with _LOCK:
28	        if name in _REGISTRY:
29	            raise ValueError(f"a study named {name!r} is already registered")
30	        _REGISTRY[name] = factory
31	
32	
33	def get_study(name: str) -> StudyDefinition:
34	    """Instantiate the registered study ``name``."""
35	    try:
36	        factory = _REGISTRY[name]
37	    except KeyError:
38	        raise KeyError(f"no study registered under {name!r}") from None
39	    return factory()
40	
41	
42	def registered_studies() -> tuple[str, ...]:
43	    return tuple(sorted(_REGISTRY))
44	
45	
46	def clear_registry() -> None:
47	    """Test helper: drop all registrations."""
48	    with _LOCK:
49	        _REGISTRY.clear()
50

1	"""Study runner — the composed, governed, end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	The phase machine (:mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.phases`) *names* the ordered stages a
4	study moves through; the firewall runners enforce the methodology; the seams provide tracing,
5	experiment tracking, data versioning, and (in the adapter's callables) reasoning. This module
6	composes them into a single runnable flow, engine-owned so the ordering and the governance
7	handoffs are guaranteed once, not re-implemented per study:
8	
9	    INGEST → BASELINE(blind, Firewall A) → [DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B)]
10	           → [ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A, shared baseline)] → REPORT
11	
12	At every step it (a) records an append-only provenance entry *before the next step runs*
13	(GOVERNANCE §3), (b) opens a trace span (observability seam), optionally (c) versions the source
14	and logs to an experiment tracker, and (d) enforces the methodological invariants structurally:
15	the baseline is built blind, the split ids are validated against the corpus, hypotheses are
16	verifiably pre-registered before confirmation, a human gate reviews before *every* confirmatory
17	step, claim-derived hypotheses are locked before the baseline exists, and the visited phase
18	sequence must equal the mode's required sequence.
19	
20	A study supplies the *domain* via a :class:`StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
21	discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, the claims source, optional feature builders); the
22	engine owns the *order, the firewalls, the gate, the provenance, the tracing, and the
23	versioning*. Reasoning enters only inside the study's own callables (which may use
24	:mod:`assay_engine.reasoning`); any such LLM output is *judgment*, so it should be wrapped as an
25	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.fence.Interpretation` and never fed back into a measurement
26	(the fence). The runner imports no adapter (ARCHITECTURE §3).
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import datetime as _dt
32	import uuid
33	from contextlib import ExitStack
34	from dataclasses import dataclass
35	from pathlib import Path
36	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Mapping
37	
38	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
39	from assay_engine.baseline.determinism import corpus_fingerprint
40	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact, BaselineBuilder
41	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord
42	from assay_engine.contracts.features import FeatureMatrix
43	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
44	from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition, StudyMode
45	from assay_engine.methodology.adjudication import (
46	    ClaimConfirmer,
47	    SourceScorecard,
48	    adjudicate_with_baseline,
49	)
50	from assay_engine.methodology.discovery import HeldOutConfirmer, subset_corpus
51	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import (
52	    ClaimBlindGuard,
53	    DiscoverConfirmSplit,
54	    FirewallViolation,
55	)
56	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisOrigin
57	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import TimestampAuthority, require_preregistered
58	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
59	from assay_engine.observability.tracing import (
60	    OtelTracer,
61	    Tracer,
62	    bootstrap_tracing,
63	    run_trace_context,
64	)
65	from assay_engine.observability.tracking import ExperimentTracker
66	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import GateDecision, GateError
67	from assay_engine.orchestration.phases import Phase, required_phases
68	from assay_engine.persistence.versioning import DataVersioner
69	from assay_engine.provenance import Clock, ProvenanceEntry, ProvenanceTrail
70	
71	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
72	
73	DiscoverFn = Callable[[Corpus], Iterable[Hypothesis]]
74	
75	
76	class IngestionError(ValueError):
77	    """Ingestion failed (the parser raised or produced an unusable corpus)."""
78	
79	
80	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
81	class GateReview:
82	    """The packet handed to a gate handler at a governance transition."""
83	
84	    gate: str
85	    frm: Phase
86	    to: Phase
87	    summary: str
88	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
89	
90	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
91	        # deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable (#113)
92	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
93	
94	
95	GateHandler = Callable[[GateReview], GateDecision]
96	
97	
98	def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
99	    """A non-interactive gate handler: approve and record the decision.
100	
101	    This is the explicit "no operator" choice — it is **not** the default, so a study cannot
102	    silently bypass human review by omitting a handler (it must consciously pass this). Replace
103	    with an operator-driven handler (e.g. one wired to the LangGraph gate interrupt/resume in
104	    :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration`) for genuine human-in-the-loop parking; the handler may
105	    return ``approved=False`` to block the transition.
106	    """
107	    return GateDecision(
108	        approved=True,
109	        gate=review.gate,
110	        reason="non-interactive auto-approval",
111	        evidence={"transition": f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}"},
112	    )
113	
114	
115	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
116	class StudyPlan:
117	    """Everything the runner needs for one run: the static definition + executable methodology.
118	
119	    ``definition`` is the adapter's binding (parser, modes, claims source, questions, optional
120	    feature builders). The callables below are the adapter's *executable* methodology, validated
121	    against the declared modes: discovery needs ``split``/``discover``/``confirm_held_out``;
122	    adjudication needs ``hypothesis_for``/``confirm_claim`` (and a ``claims_source`` on the
123	    definition).
124	    """
125	
126	    definition: StudyDefinition
127	    source: Path
128	    baseline_builder: BaselineBuilder
129	    authority: TimestampAuthority
130	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit | None = None
131	    discover: DiscoverFn | None = None
132	    confirm_held_out: HeldOutConfirmer | None = None
133	    hypothesis_for: Callable[[ClaimRecord], Hypothesis] | None = None
134	    confirm_claim: ClaimConfirmer | None = None
135	
136	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
137	        modes = self.definition.modes
138	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
139	            missing = [
140	                n
141	                for n, v in (
142	                    ("split", self.split),
143	                    ("discover", self.discover),
144	                    ("confirm_held_out", self.confirm_held_out),
145	                )
146	                if v is None
147	            ]
148	            if missing:
149	                raise ValueError(f"DISCOVERY mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
150	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
151	            missing = [
152	                n
153	                for n, v in (
154	                    ("hypothesis_for", self.hypothesis_for),
155	                    ("confirm_claim", self.confirm_claim),
156	                )
157	                if v is None
158	            ]
159	            if missing:
160	                raise ValueError(f"ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
161	
162	
163	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
164	class StudyResult:
165	    """The reproducibility package: the artifacts + the append-only trail that produced them."""
166	
167	    study: str
168	    corpus_fingerprint: str
169	    baseline: BaselineArtifact
170	    discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...]
171	    scorecard: SourceScorecard | None
172	    phases: tuple[Phase, ...]
173	    provenance: tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]
174	    source_version: str | None = None
175	    feature_matrices: tuple[FeatureMatrix, ...] = ()
176	    experiment_run_id: str | None = None
177	
178	
179	def _count_logger(key: str, n: float) -> Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]:
180	    """A typed metric-logging callback for :func:`run_study`'s best-effort tracker."""
181	
182	    def _log(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
183	        t.log_metric(rid, key, n)
184	
185	    return _log
186	
187	
188	def _now() -> _dt.datetime:
189	    # Ordering instants are a security/methodology check and ALWAYS use real wall-clock — never
190	    # a test-injected clock (that is only for deterministic provenance hashes).
191	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
192	
193	
194	def run_study(
195	    plan: StudyPlan,
196	    *,
197	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
198	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
199	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
200	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
201	    clock: Clock | None = None,
202	    secret: bytes | None = None,
203	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
204	) -> StudyResult:
205	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
206	
207	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
208	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
209	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
210	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
211	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
212	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
213	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
214	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
215	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
216	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
217	
218	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
219	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
220	    """
221	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
222	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
223	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
224	    defn = plan.definition
225	    modes = defn.modes
226	    required = required_phases(modes)
227	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
228	    visited: list[Phase] = []
229	    run_id: str | None = None
230	
231	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
232	        visited.append(phase)
233	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
234	
235	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
236	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
237	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
238	            return
239	        try:
240	            fn(tracker, run_id)
241	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
242	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
243	
244	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
245	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
246	        decision = gate_handler(review)
247	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
248	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
249	        trail.record(
250	            "gate",
251	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
252	            gate=review.gate,
253	            approved=approved,
254	            reason=reason,
255	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
256	        )
257	        if not approved:
258	            raise GateError(
259	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
260	            )
261	
262	    trail.record(
263	        "run_start",
264	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
265	        study=defn.name,
266	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
267	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
268	    )
269	    if tracker is not None:
270	        try:
271	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
272	                defn.name,
273	                {
274	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
275	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
276	                },
277	            )
278	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
279	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
280	
281	    # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
282	    # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122). Best-effort.
283	    bootstrap_tracing()
284	    corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
285	    ok = False
286	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
287	    trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
288	    try:
289	        # ---- INGEST ----
290	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
291	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
292	            try:
293	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
294	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
295	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
296	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
297	            if not corpus.units:
298	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")
299	            cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
300	            source_version: str | None = None
301	            if versioner is not None:
302	                source_version = versioner.put(str(plan.source))
303	            trail.record(
304	                "ingest",
305	                "parsed source into canonical corpus",
306	                source_fingerprint=source_fp,
307	                corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
308	                n_units=len(corpus.units),
309	                n_relations=len(corpus.relations),
310	                source_version=source_version,
311	            )
312	            # split ids must reference real corpus units (no silent partition drift, audit H4)
313	            if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
314	                assert plan.split is not None
315	                unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
316	                unknown = (set(plan.split.discovery_ids) | set(plan.split.confirm_ids)) - unit_ids
317	                if unknown:
318	                    raise FirewallViolation(
319	                        f"split references {len(unknown)} id(s) absent from the corpus "
320	                        f"(e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}) — partition would silently drop units"
321	                    )
322	            # optional dataset features (recorded + returned; the baseline builder is blind-built
323	            # from the corpus, so features are provenance/output, not a baseline input here)
324	            feature_matrices: list[FeatureMatrix] = []
325	            corpus_unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
326	            for fb in defn.feature_builders:
327	                fm = fb.build(corpus)
328	                # features must describe THIS corpus's units — no fictional or duplicate ids
329	                # (the feature-side analogue of the split-drift guard above).
330	                if len(set(fm.unit_ids)) != len(fm.unit_ids):
331	                    raise FirewallViolation(
332	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned duplicate unit_ids"
333	                    )
334	                ghost = set(fm.unit_ids) - corpus_unit_ids
335	                if ghost:
336	                    raise FirewallViolation(
337	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned {len(ghost)} unit_id(s) "
338	                        f"absent from the corpus (e.g. {sorted(ghost)[:3]})"
339	                    )
340	                feature_matrices.append(fm)
341	                trail.record(
342	                    "features",
343	                    f"built feature matrix ({len(fm.feature_names)} features)",
344	                    provides=list(fb.provides),
345	                    n_units=len(fm.unit_ids),
346	                    feature_names=list(fm.feature_names),
347	                )
348	
349	        # ---- BASELINE (blind — Firewall A). Capture the ordering instant BEFORE the build. ----
350	        baseline_instant = _now()
351	        with tracer.span("phase:BASELINE"):
352	            enter(Phase.BASELINE)
353	            guard: ClaimBlindGuard[Any] = ClaimBlindGuard()
354	            with guard.sealed():
355	                baseline = plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
356	            if baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp:
357	                raise FirewallViolation(
358	                    "baseline corpus_fingerprint does not match the ingested corpus — the baseline "
359	                    "was not built from this corpus"
360	                )
361	            trail.record(
362	                "baseline",
363	                "built blind baseline (Firewall A)",
364	                corpus_fingerprint=baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
365	                contents=sorted(baseline.contents),
366	                determinism=dict(baseline.determinism),
367	            )
368	
369	        discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = ()
370	        scorecard: SourceScorecard | None = None
371	
372	        # ---- DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B) ----
373	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
374	            assert plan.split is not None and plan.discover is not None
375	            assert plan.confirm_held_out is not None
376	            discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.discovery_ids)
377	            held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.confirm_ids)
378	
379	            with tracer.span("phase:DISCOVERY"):
380	                enter(Phase.DISCOVERY)
381	                if not discovery_corpus.units:
382	                    raise FirewallViolation("discovery partition selects no corpus units")
383	                hypotheses = list(plan.discover(discovery_corpus))  # only the discovery partition
384	                trail.record(
385	                    "discovery",
386	                    f"data surfaced {len(hypotheses)} candidate hypotheses",
387	                    n=len(hypotheses),
388	                    ids=[h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
389	                )
390	
391	            with tracer.span("phase:PREREGISTER"):
392	                enter(Phase.PREREGISTER)
393	                if not held_out.units:
394	                    raise FirewallViolation("confirmation partition selects no corpus units")
395	                preregister_instant = _now()
396	                for h in hypotheses:
397	                    if h.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
398	                        raise FirewallViolation(
399	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} is {h.origin.value!r}; the discovery "
400	                            "phase expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
401	                        )
402	                    vt = require_preregistered(
403	                        h, authority=plan.authority, not_after=preregister_instant
404	                    )
405	                    trail.record(
406	                        "preregister",
407	                        f"locked hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
408	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
409	                        digest=vt.digest,
410	                        locked_at=vt.instant.isoformat(),
411	                    )
412	                run_gate(
413	                    GateReview(
414	                        gate="review-locked-hypotheses",
415	                        frm=Phase.PREREGISTER,
416	                        to=Phase.CONFIRM,
417	                        summary="review locked hypotheses before confirmatory testing",
418	                        payload={
419	                            "hypothesis_ids": [h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
420	                            "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
421	                        },
422	                    )
423	                )
424	
425	            with tracer.span("phase:CONFIRM"):
426	                enter(Phase.CONFIRM)
427	                confirmed: list[Verdict] = []
428	                for h in hypotheses:
429	                    verdict = plan.confirm_held_out(h, held_out)  # only the held-out partition
430	                    if verdict.hypothesis_id != h.hypothesis_id:
431	                        raise FirewallViolation(
432	                            f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for "
433	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
434	                        )
435	                    confirmed.append(verdict)
436	                    trail.record(
437	                        "verdict",
438	                        f"confirmed {h.hypothesis_id!r}: {verdict.label.value}",
439	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
440	                        label=verdict.label.value,
441	                        rule=verdict.decision_rule,
442	                    )
443	                discovery_verdicts = tuple(confirmed)
444	                for lbl in ("supported", "contradicted", "indeterminate"):
445	                    n = sum(1 for v in discovery_verdicts if v.label.value == lbl)
446	                    track(_count_logger(f"discovery_{lbl}", float(n)))
447	
448	        # ---- ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A; the baseline built above is reused, blind) ----
449	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
450	            assert defn.claims_source is not None
451	            assert plan.hypothesis_for is not None and plan.confirm_claim is not None
452	            claim_records = list(defn.claims_source.claims())
453	            if not claim_records:
454	                raise ValueError(
455	                    "adjudication mode but the claims source yielded no claims — nothing to adjudicate"
456	                )
457	            prev_phase = Phase.CONFIRM if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes else Phase.BASELINE
458	            run_gate(
459	                GateReview(
460	                    gate="review-baseline-and-claims",
461	                    frm=prev_phase,
462	                    to=Phase.ADJUDICATE,
463	                    summary="review the blind baseline and the external claim set before adjudication",
464	                    payload={
465	                        "n_claims": len(claim_records),
466	                        "claim_ids": [c.claim_id for c in claim_records],
467	                        "claim_fingerprint": defn.claims_source.claim_fingerprint(),
468	                        "baseline_fingerprint": baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
469	                        "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
470	                    },
471	                )
472	            )
473	            with tracer.span("phase:ADJUDICATE"):
474	                enter(Phase.ADJUDICATE)
475	
476	                def _record_adjudication_step(h: Hypothesis, v: Verdict) -> None:
477	                    trail.record(
478	                        "preregister",
479	                        f"locked claim hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
480	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
481	                        source_claim_id=h.source_claim_id,
482	                        locked_at=h.locked_at,
483	                    )
484	                    trail.record(
485	                        "verdict",
486	                        f"adjudicated {v.hypothesis_id!r}: {v.label.value}",
487	                        hypothesis_id=v.hypothesis_id,
488	                        label=v.label.value,
489	                        rule=v.decision_rule,
490	                    )
491	
492	                # not_after = baseline_instant: a claim-derived hypothesis must have been locked
493	                # before the (shared) baseline existed. The SAME materialized claim_records that
494	                # were gated/recorded are scored (#95).
495	                scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
496	                    baseline,
497	                    claim_records,
498	                    hypothesis_for=plan.hypothesis_for,
499	                    confirm=plan.confirm_claim,
500	                    authority=plan.authority,
501	                    not_after=baseline_instant,
502	                    source_name=defn.name,
503	                    on_step=_record_adjudication_step,
504	                )
505	            with tracer.span("phase:SCORE"):
506	                enter(Phase.SCORE)
507	                trail.record(
508	                    "score",
509	                    f"source scorecard for {defn.name!r}",
510	                    n_supported=scorecard.n_supported,
511	                    n_contradicted=scorecard.n_contradicted,
512	                    n_indeterminate=scorecard.n_indeterminate,
513	                    alignment_rate=scorecard.alignment_rate,
514	                )
515	                sc = scorecard
516	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_supported", float(sc.n_supported)))
517	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_contradicted", float(sc.n_contradicted)))
518	                track(
519	                    lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_indeterminate", float(sc.n_indeterminate))
520	                )
521	                ar = sc.alignment_rate
522	                if ar is not None:
523	                    track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "alignment_rate", float(ar)))
524	
525	        # ---- REPORT ----
526	        with tracer.span("phase:REPORT"):
527	            enter(Phase.REPORT)
528	            trail.record(
529	                "report",
530	                "assembled reproducibility package",
531	                n_discovery_verdicts=len(discovery_verdicts),
532	                has_scorecard=scorecard is not None,
533	            )
534	
535	        if tuple(visited) != required:
536	            raise FirewallViolation(
537	                f"visited phase sequence {[p.name for p in visited]} != required "
538	                f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
539	            )
540	        trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
541	        ok = True
542	    except Exception as exc:
543	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
544	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
545	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
546	        try:
547	            trail.record(
548	                "run_failed",
549	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
550	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
551	                message=str(exc)[:2000],
552	                last_phase=last_phase,
553	            )
554	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — never mask the original failure
555	            pass
556	        raise
557	    finally:
558	        trace_ctx.close()
559	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
560	            try:
561	                # a failed run must be distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110)
562	                tracker.end_run(run_id, status="FINISHED" if ok else "FAILED")
563	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — best-effort cleanup
564	                pass
565	
566	    return StudyResult(
567	        study=defn.name,
568	        corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
569	        baseline=baseline,
570	        discovery_verdicts=discovery_verdicts,
571	        scorecard=scorecard,
572	        phases=tuple(visited),
573	        provenance=trail.entries,
574	        source_version=source_version,
575	        feature_matrices=tuple(feature_matrices),
576	        experiment_run_id=run_id,
577	    )

1	"""Baseline builder contract + a deterministic artifact wrapper.
2	
3	This module fixes the *shape* of a baseline artifact and the build contract. The engine ships
4	the dataset-agnostic building blocks for a baseline (similarity/distance, descriptive stats,
5	the determinism harness in :mod:`assay_engine.baseline`); the choice-bearing builder that
6	assembles them — which embedding model, which clustering, which graph — encodes dataset
7	decisions and is supplied by a study's adapter as a :class:`BaselineBuilder` (ADR-0002).
8	
9	Firewall A is enforced here structurally: :meth:`BaselineBuilder.build` accepts a
10	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.firewalls.ClaimBlindGuard` and never accepts a claims
11	source — a baseline simply cannot be handed the claims it must stay blind to.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
17	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol
18	
19	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
20	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ExternalClaimsSource
21	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
22	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import ClaimBlindGuard
23	
24	
25	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
26	class BaselineArtifact:
27	    """A deterministic, versioned empirical model of the corpus — the one privileged object.
28	
29	    Concrete contents (embedding matrices, similarity graphs, cluster assignments, etc.) live
30	    under ``contents`` keyed by builder name. ``determinism`` records the seeds, input hashes,
31	    and model/version strings needed to reproduce it.
32	    """
33	
34	    corpus_fingerprint: str
35	    contents: Mapping[str, Any]
36	    determinism: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
37	
38	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
39	        object.__setattr__(self, "contents", freeze_mapping(self.contents))
40	        object.__setattr__(self, "determinism", freeze_mapping(self.determinism))
41	
42	
43	class BaselineBuilder(Protocol):
44	    """Build a :class:`BaselineArtifact` from a corpus, blind to any external claims."""
45	
46	    def build(
47	        self, corpus: Corpus, *, claim_guard: ClaimBlindGuard[ExternalClaimsSource]
48	    ) -> BaselineArtifact:
49	        """Construct the baseline. Must run with ``claim_guard.sealed()`` in force."""
50	        ...
51

1	"""The two firewalls, enforced as runtime primitives (ADR-0005, METHODOLOGY.md §2).
2	
3	Both separations are enforced in code and exercised by tests — never asserted only in prose.
4	
5	Firewall A — claim-blindness:
6	    The baseline must be built blind to external claims. The *structural* guarantee is that
7	    the baseline builder is never handed an ``ExternalClaimsSource`` (its ``build`` signature
8	    takes only a ``Corpus``) and that ``StudyDefinition`` refuses a claims source outside
9	    adjudication mode. On top of that, :class:`ClaimBlindGuard` gives the claims source
10	    *custody*: hand the source to the guard, and the only way to obtain it is
11	    ``release()``/``access()``, which raise while the guard is sealed. Baseline construction
12	    runs inside ``sealed()``; adjudication runs after it.
13	
14	    Caveat (audit pass 1, issue #1): the guard cannot police a baseline builder that ignores
15	    it and reads external judgments smuggled into ``Corpus`` metadata/relations — that path
16	    is closed by the adapter contract (see schema.py), not by this guard. The guard is
17	    custodial defense-in-depth over the type-level separation, not the sole barrier.
18	
19	Firewall B — discover/confirm separation:
20	    The data used to discover a hypothesis must not be the data used to confirm it.
21	    :class:`DiscoverConfirmSplit` holds disjoint, non-empty discovery and confirmation id
22	    sets and refuses to confirm on any id the discovery phase touched (or on an empty set).
23	"""
24	
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	from contextlib import contextmanager
28	from dataclasses import dataclass
29	from typing import Generic, Iterable, Iterator, TypeVar
30	
31	C = TypeVar("C")
32	
33	
34	class FirewallViolation(RuntimeError):
35	    """Raised when a methodological firewall is breached at runtime."""
36	
37	
38	class ClaimBlindGuard(Generic[C]):
39	    """Firewall A. While sealed, the held claims source is unreachable.
40	
41	    Custodial usage (preferred) — the guard owns the source, so the baseline pipeline holds
42	    the guard rather than the source and cannot read claims while sealed::
43	
44	        guard = ClaimBlindGuard(claims_source)
45	        with guard.sealed():
46	            corpus = parser.parse(source)            # baseline build — claims unreachable
47	            baseline = build_baseline(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
48	        claims = guard.release()                      # adjudication — guard released
49	
50	    ``access(obj)`` remains for defensive checks at call sites that hold a reference directly:
51	    it returns ``obj`` when unsealed and raises while sealed.
52	    """
53	
54	    def __init__(self, claims_source: C | None = None) -> None:
55	        self._sealed = False
56	        self._claims_source = claims_source
57	
58	    @property
59	    def is_sealed(self) -> bool:
60	        return self._sealed
61	
62	    @contextmanager
63	    def sealed(self) -> Iterator["ClaimBlindGuard[C]"]:
64	        prior = self._sealed
65	        self._sealed = True
66	        try:
67	            yield self
68	        finally:
69	            self._sealed = prior
70	
71	    def release(self) -> C:
72	        """Return the custodial claims source, or raise while sealed / if none was given."""
73	        if self._sealed:
74	            raise FirewallViolation(
75	                "Firewall A: external claims released during blind baseline construction"
76	            )
77	        if self._claims_source is None:
78	            raise FirewallViolation("Firewall A: no claims source is under this guard's custody")
79	        return self._claims_source
80	
81	    def access(self, claims_source: C) -> C:
82	        """Return ``claims_source`` only if the guard is not sealed; else raise."""
83	        if self._sealed:
84	            raise FirewallViolation(
85	                "Firewall A: external claims accessed during blind baseline construction"
86	            )
87	        return claims_source
88	
89	
90	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
91	class DiscoverConfirmSplit:
92	    """Firewall B. Disjoint discovery / confirmation id partitions for unit-level claims."""
93	
94	    discovery_ids: frozenset[str]
95	    confirm_ids: frozenset[str]
96	
97	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
98	        overlap = self.discovery_ids & self.confirm_ids
99	        if overlap:
100	            raise FirewallViolation(
101	                f"Firewall B: discovery and confirmation partitions overlap on "
102	                f"{len(overlap)} id(s), e.g. {sorted(overlap)[:3]}"
103	            )
104	        # An empty confirmation partition means there is nothing held out to confirm on; a
105	        # unit-level held-out split with no held-out object is invalid by construction. The
106	        # genuine no-holdout case must use the whole-corpus null path (audit pass 1, issue #5).
107	        if not self.confirm_ids:
108	            raise FirewallViolation(
109	                "Firewall B: confirmation partition is empty — a unit-level held-out split "
110	                "must hold out at least one object (use whole-corpus confirmation otherwise)"
111	            )
112	
113	    @classmethod
114	    def from_partition(
115	        cls, discovery_ids: Iterable[str], confirm_ids: Iterable[str]
116	    ) -> "DiscoverConfirmSplit":
117	        return cls(frozenset(discovery_ids), frozenset(confirm_ids))
118	
119	    def assert_confirm_only(self, ids: Iterable[str]) -> None:
120	        """Raise if ``ids`` is empty or any of it was seen during discovery (double-dip)."""
121	        ids = frozenset(ids)
122	        if not ids:
123	            raise FirewallViolation(
124	                "Firewall B: confirmatory test evaluated zero held-out objects — a verdict "
125	                "cannot be produced from an empty confirmation set (audit pass 1, issue #5)"
126	            )
127	        leaked = ids & self.discovery_ids
128	        if leaked:
129	            raise FirewallViolation(
130	                f"Firewall B: confirmatory test touched {len(leaked)} discovery id(s) — "
131	                f"double-dipping, e.g. {sorted(leaked)[:3]}"
132	            )
133	        unknown = ids - self.confirm_ids
134	        if unknown:
135	            raise FirewallViolation(
136	                f"Firewall B: confirmatory test used {len(unknown)} id(s) outside the "
137	                f"confirmation partition, e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}"
138	            )
139
